Best early deals to shop during Amazon’s Big Spring Sale

Every early deal I recommend below is at least 20% off and has a 4.0-star average rating from hundreds of reviews on Amazon at minimum. I prioritized products the NBC Select staff has tested, myself included, and picked popular brands we’ve previously covered. If any deals are Prime member exclusives, I marked them accordingly.
Apple AirTag (4 Pack)

I have an AirTag on my keys, as well as in my wallet and suitcase, so I always know where my belongings are. The little trackers seamlessly pair with the FindMy app on your iPhone or iPad, showing each AirTag's location on a map and letting you ring the device, which has a built-in speaker. AirTags, which are water- and dust-resistant, come with replaceable batteries that last for over a year, according to the brand.
ZeroWater 32-Cup Water Filter Dispenser

Not only does this 32-cup water dispenser have a built-in, five-stage filter, but it also comes with a meter that monitors purity levels and tells you when you need to replace the filter. You can keep it in the refrigerator or on your counter with the spout facing toward you to easily dispense water.
Flamingo Shaving Razor Set

I’ve been loyal to Flamingo’s razor for years because it gives me a close shave, smoothly glides across my skin, easily pivots and never leaves behind irritation. The head has five blades and an aloe-shea butter strip, and the weighted handle has a non-slip grip. This set comes with four cartridge refills, a handle and a shower holder that suctions to tile.
Levoit LVAC-200 Cordless Vacuum

Levoit’s lightweight, cordless vacuum has a five-stage filtration system and it gives you up to 50 minutes of runtime on a full battery, according to the brand. Its head is designed with a tangle-resistant roller, and it lays almost completely flat to help you clean under furniture. You can also convert it into a handheld vacuum and add the included crevice and pet tools, as well as keep the long stick attached to reach high areas like above doorframes.
Hydro Flask Travel Water Bottle

Hydro Flask makes some of our favorite water bottles, and this 32-ounce option has a tapered base that’s compatible with most cupholders. The dishwasher-safe bottle is made from stainless steel and it’s insulated, so it keeps beverages cold for up to 24-hours, according to the brand. Its lid has a flip-up straw, and when you click it shut, it’s leak-resistant.
Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Plus

Once you plug Amazon’s Fire Stick into your TV’s HDMI port, you can enjoy your favorite shows, movies and music across streaming platforms, as well as play Xbox games without a console and control compatible smart home devices. The Fire Stick comes with an Amazon Alexa Voice Remote, and it automatically optimizes visuals for 4K TVs.
Keurig K-Compact Single-Serve Coffee Maker

Using this single-serve coffee maker is the first thing I think about when I wake up every morning. It heats up and brews beverages in under a minute, according to the brand, and you can choose from three cup sizes: 6, 8 and 10 ounces. I only fill up the 36-ounce removable water reservoir every few days, and I’m able to remove the drip tray to fit travel mugs under the spout. As its name implies, the coffee machine, which is compatible with all types of K-Cup pods, is quite compact, so it takes up very little space on my small counter.
Medicube Zero Pore Pad

About five months ago, I started using these pore pads every other night and since then, my skin looks brighter and feels smoother. I’ve also noticed a visible reduction in pore size, especially in my t-zone. The pads are soaked in an exfoliating solution made from BHAs and AHAs, and they’re dual-sided: the textured side, which you use first, gently buffs away dead skin cells, while the soft side hydrates and soothes. After I use the pads, I let my skin absorb the solution, which only takes a few minutes since it’s so lightweight. Medicube’s Zero Pore Pads come in a jar with tweezers that nest in the lid, letting you grab one pad at a time.
Toacsucp Candle Warmer Lamp

Put a candle under this warmer’s heat bulb, and as its wax melts, your favorite scent will waft throughout your home, no open flame needed. You can adjust the lamp’s height, and it has a built-in dimmer and timer. The candle warmer also doubles as home decor due to its wood base and floral-looking glass lampshade.
Coway Airmega AP-1512HH(W)

I have severe seasonal allergies and three shedding pets, so this air purifier, which I’ve used for about three years, runs at all times in my apartment. It has a three-stage filtration system (a pre-filter, deodorization filter and HEPA filter), a timer, multiple modes and a built-in air quality sensor. You can manually choose from one of three fan speeds, or set the air purifier to auto mode, causing it to automatically adjust fan speed depending on your indoor air quality. I find the appliance to be very quiet — I only hear it when the fan speed goes up to the highest level, like while I’m cooking.
Blink Video Doorbell (newest model)

See, hear and speak to visitors with Blink’s Video Doorbell, which runs on AA batteries that last for about two years, according to the brand. After pairing the Amazon Alexa-enabled device with the brand’s app, you can watch livestream footage that gives you a wide head-to-toe view. The app also sends you motion detection alerts and saves clips in a cloud with an additional subscription.
Kasa Smart Plug (2 pack)

Connect devices like fans, small kitchen appliances and lamps to this smart plug, which lets you control them via Kasa’s app or voice commands. You can also set on/off schedules and timers through the app, and turn on away mode to save energy when you’re not home. The plug is compact, so it won’t block your second outlet.
Head & Shoulders Anti-Dandruff Shampoo and Conditioner (2 pack)

Head & Shoulders’ 2-in-1 shampoo and conditioner cleanses and hydrates hair while providing itch relief, reducing dryness and eliminating visible flakes for up to 72 hours, according to the brand. It’s formulated with zinc, a gentle ingredient that helps control the yeast that can contribute to dandruff, according to dermatologists we’ve talked to. The 2-in-1 is safe for color-treated hair.
Samsonite Freeform Hardside Carry-On

I’ve owned my Samsonite luggage for years, and this carry-on has all the essential features you want while packing for a trip: internal organization pockets, easy-glide spinner wheels, the option to expand the bag by an extra inch and a built-in TSA-approved lock. It’s hard textured shell is also durable and scratch-resistant, according to the brand.
Shark StainForce Cordless Portable Stain-Destroying System

The Shark StainForce is a rechargeable, cordless spot cleaner that comes with everything you need to remove dirt, spills and pet accidents from carpets and upholstered furniture. First, you spray stains with the included solution, and then you use the tool’s brush to scrub the area before turning it on and suctioning the stain away. Everything nests inside the included caddy, and you get a charging cable and crevice tool, too.
Merach Vibration Plate Exercise Machine

If social media has convinced you to buy a vibration plate for workouts, consider trying this option from Merach. It has a LED touchscreen, 99 speed levels, three vibration zones and multiple modes, plus it comes with resistance bands you can clip on to do arm exercises. The plate also connects to your phone via Bluetooth to play music.
Waterpik Cordless Advanced 2.0 Flosser

Waterpik’s water flosser is one of our team’s favorite models because it’s cordless — its battery lasts for up to four weeks, according to the brand, so it won’t hog an outlet or take up space on your bathroom’s counter, plus you can throw it in your toiletry while traveling. The water flosser, which earned the American Dental Association’s Seal of Acceptance, offers 45 seconds of use every time you fill up its water reservoir. It also has three pressure settings and comes with four interchangeable tips that rotate 360 degrees, making it easy to each every part of your mouth.
Olay Total Effects Facial Moisturizing Lotion SPF 30

Olay’s lotion is made with ingredients like niacinamide, antioxidants and vitamins C, A, B5 and E to hydrate skin while brightening tone and smoothing texture over time, according to the brand. It also protects you from UV rays since it has SPF 30. The lotion comes in a pump bottle and it absorbs quickly so you can layer makeup over it.
Dreo Nomad One S Tower Fan

Dreo’s oscillating bladeless fan quietly cools your space, so it won’t disturb you while you’re watching TV or sleeping. The fan has four modes, four speeds and a built-in timer. It comes with a remote, or you can control it using voice commands and Dreo’s app once you pair it with Wi-Fi.
All-Clad HA1 Hard Anodized Nonstick Fry Pan Set

You get one 8-inch and one 10-inch fry pan in this set. The cookware has an aluminum and stainless steel base to evenly and consistently heat food, a nonstick coating so your meal easily releases from the surface, and sturdy handle. These pans are compatible with all stovetops, oven-safe up to 500 degrees Fahrenheit and dishwasher-safe, according to the brand.
Sony WI-C100 Wireless In-Ear Bluetooth Headphones

These headphones give you the benefits of earbuds and corded headphones in one. Like earbuds, they connect to your device via Bluetooth, but like headphones, the earpieces are connected via a cord (it goes behind your neck), making them hard to lose. The headphones, which are splash- and sweat-resistant, give you up to 25 hours of battery life, according to the brand. They also have a built-in microphone as well as buttons to pause, play, skip tracks and adjust the volume.

title: Emily Bader, Logan Lerman to Star in ‘13 Going on 30’ Reboot
summary: Emily Bader and Logan Lerman are ready to be thirty, flirty and thriving.
story: Emily Bader and Logan Lerman are ready to be thirty, flirty and thriving.
Netflix has announced a reboot of the 2004 romantic comedy “13 Going on 30,” with Bader and Lerman taking over as the leads. The original film starred Jennifer Garner and Mark Ruffalo.

On March 24, the streaming giant tweeted the casting news and announced that Garner will be an executive producer. The “13 Going on 30” reboot will be directed by Bretty Haley, who recently worked with Bader on the hit Netflix flick “People We Meet on Vacation,” based on the Emily Henry novel of the same name.

In a statement about teaming up with Bader again and rebooting the beloved movie, Haley said in a statement, “‘13 Going on 30’ is one of those rare, perfect films. Funny, emotional, deeply human, with unforgettable performances from Jennifer Garner, Mark Ruffalo, and Judy Greer. I’m a longtime fan, so stepping into this reimagining comes with tremendous responsibility. Jennifer Garner being on board as an executive producer, after playing such a big part of what made the original special, is especially meaningful.”

He added, “I also couldn’t be more excited to reunite with Emily Bader after ‘People We Meet on Vacation.’ She and the amazingly talented Logan Lerman are a magical pairing. I feel incredibly lucky to be trusted with something that means so much to so many people.”
At this time, Netflix has not shared information about the reboot’s plot or when it would take place.

The 2004 version is set in 1987 New Jersey, where fans are introduced to 13-year-old Jenna Rink, played by Christa B. Allen. The relatable teenager dreams of becoming popular and being accepted into the “Tom-Tom” clique at her school despite her best friend Matty Flamhaff, played by Sean Marquette, assuring her that she’s better off without them.

On her 13th birthday, Jenna throws a party at her house and Matty gifts her a custom dollhouse that he sprinkles with “magic wishing dust.” After being embarrassed by the cool kids and having her party ruined, Jenna wishes to be “thirty and flirty and thriving,” just like the model she sees on the cover of her favorite magazine. Her wish comes true and she wakes up in the body of a 30-year-old successful magazine editor, portrayed by Garner. But she soon realizes being an adult isn’t as glamorous as she thought it would be. She also wants to rekindle her friendship with adult Matty, portrayed by Ruffalo, after learning the two had a falling out.
Judy Greer, Andy Serkis, Jim Gaffigan and a young Brie Larson also starred in the film.

“13 Going on 30,” featuring a “Thriller” dance sequence, ‘80s fashion and makeup and a Billy Joel needle drop, has continued to delight rom-com fans over the years. Garner has also frequently reflected on her time filming the movie and shared throwback photos from the set on Instagram.
In July 2025, she celebrated Greer, whom she has remained close friends with, turning 50.

“We were friends before taking pictures was a thing, but there is still a little documentation. 266 months of ‘JG’ and 600 months of @missjudygreer. Happy 50th, Judy! I love you,” she captioned a slideshow of pictures that included snaps of her and Greer as Jenna and Tom-Tom.
The post also included a screenshot from 2024 of the two on a video call with Ruffalo when the trio reunited to celebrate the movie’s 20th anniversary.

title: See the 'Moana' Live-Action Actors Side-by-Side with the Animated Stars
summary: Who's playing who in Moana? See the actors side by side with the original actors.
story: The live-action "Moana" is coming this summer, with the stars suited up and ready to prove how far they'll go.
Disney released the first trailer on March 23, giving a closer look at the characters' transformations — and at Dwayne Johnson rocking demigod Maui's long, curly hair.
The movie is set to premiere on July 10, a decade after the Oscar-nominated original, Disney said in a June 2025 press release. The cast began filming in summer 2024.

Auli’i Cravalho, the voice of Moana in the original film, said in a May 2023 Instagram post that she would not play the lead in the live-action film. Now, Cravalho is an executive producer on the live-action project.

“I’m truly honored to pass this baton to the next woman of Pacific Island descent to honor our incredible Pacific peoples, cultures, and communities that help inspire her story, and I look forward to all the beautiful Pacific representation to come. Mahalo,” Cravalho said.
Cravalho told TODAY.com in a January 2024 interview that she felt it was important to find a new lead who was closer to 16, the age of the fictional character.

“I love that the ‘Moana’ universe is growing but when I think of a live-action remake, I think it’s important that that we stay true to character. You find someone who is near to 16, you find someone who has a beautiful singing voice, you find someone who is unknown, you find someone who is of the Pacific islands,” she said. “When I talk about representation, I want to throw the door wide open. I want there to be Pacific Island representation in front as well as behind the camera.”
Catherine Laga’aia, a 17-year-old Australian actor of Samoan heritage, is stepping in to the leading role of Moana, Disney announced in June 2024.
Here’s a look at the 2026 "Moana" live-action characters compared to the 2016 animated film.
Moana (Catherine Laga’aia)
In the trailer, Catherine Laga’aia is dressed in Moana's legendary outfit, accessorized with a flower in her hair and her heart of Te Fiti necklace.
When Disney announced the lead role, Laga’aia reflected on her own heritage and admiration for Moana.
“I’m really excited to embrace this character because Moana is one of my favorites,” the 17-year-old Sydney, Australia, native said in the press release.

“My grandfather comes from Fa’aala, Palauli, in Savai’i. And my grandmother is from Leulumoega Tuai on the main island of ‘Upolu in Samoa," she continued. "I’m honored to have an opportunity to celebrate Samoa and all Pacific Island peoples, and to represent young girls who look like me.”
Maui (Dwayne Johnson)
Johnson, who serves as the original voice of demigod Maui in "Moana" and "Moana 2", makes his return for the live-action film. He is also a producer for "Moana."
He wore a curly-haired wig to recreate Maui's look, and of course replicated his full body tattoos.
When the film was announced, Johnson reflected on his Samoan heritage in a video filmed in Hawaii.
“As many of you know ... we found so much inspiration for Maui in the mana and the presence of my late grandfather, the legendary High Chief Peter Maivia,” Johnson said in a video announcement.
Later, he shared some behind-the-scenes clips from the production on Instagram.
"This means everything to us 🤎 🌊," he wrote.
Chief Tui (John Tui)
Chief Tui, Moana's father, protects and leads Moana's home base of Motunui, a fictional Polynesian island.
Temuera Morrison played Chief Tui in the 2016 animated film, with Christopher Jackson serving as his singing voice.
John Tui, an actor from Auckland, New Zealand, has taken on the role for the live-action film. He's seen in the trailer proudly gazing at Moana as she looks out into the sea for what is yet to come.
Sina (Frankie Adams)
Moana's unwavering mother is played by Frankie Adams in the live-action remake. Nicole Scherzinger was the original voice of Sina.
Upon the film's announcement, Adams posted on Instagram, congratulating Laga’aia and her co-stars.
"So honored and grateful to be part of this team! This is truly surreal. Ok ok I'm off to have another joyful cry and get ready for my trip to Montunui!" she wrote.
Adams is briefly in the live-action trailer, kissing her daughter on the head as Moana embarks on her next voyage.
In an interview with Aloha State Daily in January, Adams reflected on her upcoming movie projects, one of them being "Moana" live-action.

“You must take tissues because the amount you cry — from joy and pride — is quite insane,” Adams said. “I really hope and I feel that people are going to feel really represented when they see that and feel a sense of home.”
Gramma Tala (Rena Owen)
Moana's spiritual mentor, her grandmother, is played by Rena Owen in the live-action movie.
The original voice of Gramma Tala from the 2016 animated film was Rachel House.
From long walks on the beach with her granddaughter to providing wisdom, Gramma Tala spends her last days on Earth to tell Moana, "The Ocean chose you."
Owen shared the trailer on Instagram and wrote: "Can't wait for you all to experience Disney's #moana in theatres July 10! #polynesian #cheehoo 🌴🌀"

title: Why Aren’t Emily Osment, Mitchell Musso in the ‘Hannah Montana’ Special? What the Actors Said
summary: “Hannah Montana” stars Emily Osment and Mitchel Musso missed the 20th anniversary special, but spoke about what the show means to them.
story: The “Hannah Montana” 20th anniversary special is officially here, but two key players from the Disney show are missing in action.

Emily Osment and Mitchel Musso, who starred as Miley Stewart's best friends, Lilly and Oliver, do not appear in the special, which dropped on Disney+ March 24. But they both have spoken out about their experience being part of the hit series to mark 20 years since it premiered in March 2006.
Osment posted an Instagram video explaining that she couldn’t take part in the special because she is shooting her CBS comedy, “Georgie & Mandy’s First Marriage.”

“‘Hannah Montana’ changed my life, it gave me a lifelong respect for this medium of comedy, it taught me discipline, patience, timing and respect working in an adult space so young,” she captioned an Instagram video of her and “Georgie & Mandy’s First Marriage” co-star Montana Jordan on March 23 from the show’s set.
In the video, Osment confirmed she was “not able to be a part of the 20-year reunion because we are here shooting our show." The "Young Sheldon" spinoff is currently airing its second season.
Osment, who after "Hannah Montana" went on to star in "Young & Hungry" and "Young Sheldon," said she feels grateful for all the support she’s received from fans.

“I can’t tell you what your sweet messages mean to me and how lucky I feel to have been apart of this once in a generation goliath of a television show,” she wrote. “Thank you for letting me into your living rooms and I hope to still be there many years from now. Would never be where I am without you guys, working on another beautiful show I love so dearly. With all my heart, THANK YOU.”
Musso, meanwhile, shared a series of photos from his time on “Hannah Montana,” along with a lengthy post detailing his love for the show. He did not share why he was not part of the special.

“‘Hannah Montana’ wrapped around my heart and never really let go. We literally grew up with ya’ll — long days, crazy schedules, learning lines, cracking up between takes, and figuring out life while the cameras rolled,” he wrote.
“It taught me so many wonderful values, but the most important ‘to me’ is that laughter can get you through the tough days. That confidence still sticks with me every single day.”
Musso, who in later seasons played Lilly's love interest on the show, referenced Osment's post while noting how gratifying it is to see their viewers grow into adults.

“To all of you who grew up right alongside us… your friends were our friends too. Like my Lillypop said on her post, so many of you building families of your own and sharing this wonderful story with your kids is a gift beyond words. It’s been the coolest part of HM,” he wrote.
Musso again acknowledged Osment, as well as Miley Cyrus, Billy Ray Cyrus and fellow cast members Moises Arias and Jason Earles.

“Miley, Emily, Jason, Billy Ray, Moises, and the whole crew at Disney — you became my family through all the real, messy, beautiful moments. Thank you for believing in a goofy kid from Texas who wore some questionable outfits with a haircut like that,” he wrote.
“To all of you, thank you for welcoming me into your homes and hearts 20 years ago and still allowing me to be here today. I’m honored we get to share all of this together.”

title: 'Bridgerton' Reveals Season 5 Will Spotlight Its 1st Leading Queer Couple. What Happens In Francesca's Book?
summary: What happens in Francesca's season of Bridgerton and the book by Julia Quinn? Book spoilers give away what happens next.
story: The star of "Bridgerton" Season 5 is officially Francesca, Netflix revealed March 24, marking the first season led by a queer couple  and another major departure from the order of the Julia Quinn books that inspired it.

Season 5 is officially in production, the streamer announced on X, accompanied by a teaser video revealing that the two romantic leads will be Hannah Dodd's Francesca Bridgerton and Masali Baduza's Michaela Stirling.
"A certain countess shall find love again," the caption teased.
In the clip, Francesca and Michaela stand together on a balcony overlooking the countryside, and after sharing a glimpse at each other, gently touch hands.

"Bridgerton" showrunner Jess Brownell previously confirmed that the already renewed Seasons 5 and 6 will be led by Eloise and Francesca, but she didn't confirm which character pertains to which season.

Now, Francesca's season is officially leapfrogging that of the character's elder sister Eloise, played by Claudia Jessie. In Quinn's "Bridgerton" books, Eloise's story is told in Book 5, "To Sir Phillip With Love," followed by Francesca's in Book 6, "When He Was Wicked."
This isn't the first time the show has gone out of order. While Benedict's season was told in Season 4, he's the star of Book 3, "An Offer From a Gentleman."

But this is the first time the show's leading couple will be queer, a change fans have known since the Season 3 finale introduced a gender-swapped version of Francesca's love interest from the book, Michael Stirling, as Michaela Stirling. The new couple already has a ship name, hailed by Netflix itself: "Franchaela."
Here's what to know about Season 5, including spoilers from the book.
What Will Happen in Season 5 of 'Bridgerton'?
Warning: This section contains spoilers for the ending of "Bridgerton" Season 4.
Season 5 will pick up two years after the death of Francesca's husband, John Stirling (Victor Alli), who suddenly died in Season 4, Episode 6.

After his death, Francesca was distraught and leaned heavily on Michaela, John's cousin. Though their relationship started off rocky, with Dodd previously telling TODAY.com that Michaela “stirs something up” in Francesca, they mended fences after having a candid conversation about their differences and their shared love for John.
After his death, Francesca asks Michaela to stay in London with her as the search begins for the next heir to the Kilmartin line.

“If you would like it, I shall stay,” Michaela responds with a smile. But after Francesca grabs her hand and says, “I feel very close to you, Michaela,” Michaela pulls away, and in the next scene, has suddenly departed London.
“She has packed her belongings and left,” a maid tells Francesca, marking how both characters were left off in Season 4.
In Season 5, though, Francesca will find herself in search of a new partner, for "practical reasons," after navigating her grief. Then, Michaela will return.

The official synopsis reads, “Two years after losing her beloved husband John, Francesca (Hannah Dodd) decides to reenter the marriage mart for practical reasons. But when John’s cousin Michaela (Masali Baduza) returns to London to tend to the Kilmartin estate, Fran’s complicated feelings will have her questioning whether to stick to her pragmatic intentions or pursue her inner passions.”
What Happens In Francesca's Book, 'When He Was Wicked'?
Warning: This section contains spoilers to Julia Quinn's "When He Was Wicked," the inspiration for the forthcoming Season 5 of "Bridgerton."

The beginning of "When He Was Wicked" establishes some of the events viewers have already seen play out on screen: Francesca, deeply in love with her husband, has her heart broken by his sudden death.

In the book, Michaela Stirling is Michael Stirling, and after his cousin's death and after Francesca is confirmed not to be pregnant, he becomes the Earl of Kilmartin. But he has a secret: He's been in love with Francesca since he first met her, at a party celebrating her engagement to John.
Wanting to avoid her and his complicated feelings, Michael leaves.
Four years later, Francesca decides she wants to get re-married just so she can have a child, thinking she already had her great love with John.
At this time, she reconnects with Michael, and they have a yearning, push-pull dynamic defined by attraction, seduction and heaps of guilt as Francesca finds herself falling for Michael.
After several steamy encounters, they decide to marry. In the final scene, Francesca visits John's grave and finally admits she loves Michael, which he overhears, sealing their happily ever after.

title: Mel Schilling, Star of ‘Married at First Sight,’ Dies at 54 After Revealing Terminal Colon Cancer
summary: Mel Schilling, the dating coach on the British and Australian versions of “Married at First Sight,” has died. She'd been diagnosed with colon cancer.
story: Mel Schilling, who appeared as a dating coach on the British and Australian versions of “Married at First Sight,” has died, her family announced. She was 54.

In one of her last social media posts earlier this month, Schilling revealed the colon cancer she'd been diagnosed with in 2023 could no longer be treated. The tumor, which was “the size of a lemon,” was discovered during a scan, she said. It was removed, but the disease later returned and spread to her lungs and brain.
On her Instagram page on Tuesday, March 24, her husband wrote that she passed away peacefully and surrounded by love. Schilling is also survived by her 10-year-old daughter, Madison.

“In her final moments, when I thought cancer had taken away her ability to speak, she ushered me closer and whispered a message for Maddie and me that will sustain me for the rest of my life,” her husband, Gareth Brisbane, wrote.

“This is a woman who, through two years of chemotherapy, when she could barely lift her head from the pillow, never complained and never stopped showing courage, grace, compassion and empathy, and never missed a day of filming.”
He added that his wife became a new mom and a TV star at 42 “and nailed both.”

“Life can be beautiful, and life can be incredibly cruel. But ultimately, life is fleeting, fragile, and tomorrow is promised to no one. If you can do anything to honour Mel, please live life to the full, love your people well, and try not to sweat the small stuff,” Brisbane wrote.
“I had 15 wonderful years with my soulmate, and it was the privilege of my life to be by her side. For that, I will be forever thankful.”
Schilling’s colon cancer tumor, which she called “Terry,” was initially successfully removed, but the cancer returned and metastasized to her lungs, she revealed.

When she began experiencing headaches and numbness on her right side, tests showed the cancer had spread to the left side of her brain. Schilling said her oncology team told her “there is nothing further they can do."
“If I could leave you with one thing, it would simply be this: if something doesn’t feel right, please get it checked out. It might just save your life,” Schilling wrote.
Her colon cancer symptoms included severe stomach cramps, and Schilling was initially told she had constipation before a scan revealed the tumor, the BBC reported.

title: Where Do Miley Cyrus and Her Dad Billy Ray Cyrus Stand Now?
summary: How Miley Cyrus' complicated relationship with dad Billy Ray Cyrus has evolved over the years and where they are now.
story: Miley Cyrus' complicated relationship with dad Billy Ray Cyrus has made plenty of headlines over the years, but it seems the two have mended their relationship so much they've become nostalgic about their past.
The two entertainers will reunite onscreen in an hourlong “Hannah Montana” 20th anniversary special airing on Disney+ on March 24.

The pair’s onscreen reunion comes after Miley Cyrus reportedly stopped speaking to her father for several years in the midst of her parents’ 2022 divorce, according to an interview she gave to Variety earlier this year.
In February 2024, the 33-year-old "Flowers" singer declined to thank her father when she won her first two Grammy awards.
She later acknowledged the pair had "challenges over the years" in a statement on Instagram in May 2025, but said her family had now become her "priority above all else."
Then, in September 2025, Miley Cyrus surprised fans when she released an emotional song, “Secrets,” as a “peace offering” to her once estranged father, per an Instagram caption.
The younger Cyrus told NPR at the time the song acted as "musical medicine" to help father and daughter repair their tattered bond.
“It really helped build a bridge between him and I and reconnecting with each other,” she said.
Ahead of Miley Cyrus and Billy Ray Cyrus’ onscreen reunion later this month, let’s take a look back at their close and complicated relationship.
November 1992: Miley Cyrus Is Born the Same Year Her Dad Has a No. 1 Song

Billy Ray Cyrus and then-wife Tish Cyrus welcomed Miley Cyrus on Nov. 23, 1992, the same year Billy Ray Cyrus' hit song, “Achy Breaky Heart,” became the No. 1 song on Billboard's Hot Country Songs chart in the United States.

Billy Ray Cyrus and Tish Cyrus went on to welcome a son, Braison, and another daughter, Noah, together. Billy Ray Cyrus also adopted Tish Cyrus' children Brandi Cyrus and Trace Cyrus from her earlier marriage to Baxter Neal Helson.
March 2006: ‘Hannah Montana' Premieres on the Disney Channel

After appearing in a few small roles, including guest spots on Billy Ray Cyrus’ medical drama “Doc,” Miley Cyrus found fame alongside her dad when the pair co-star together on the hit Disney series “Hannah Montana,” which aired from 2006 to 2011.

The series told the story of a fictional teenager named Miley Stewart (Miley Cyrus) who lived a double life as a pop star named Hannah Montana. The character’s father, Robby (Billy Ray Cyrus), was a retired country music singer who wrote all her hits and managed her career.

According to Variety, toward the end of the show’s run, Billy Ray Cyrus’ relationship with Miley Cyrus’s mom, Tish Cyrus, had begun to unravel, which caused his relationship with his daughter to fray.
February 2011: Billy Ray Cyrus Says 'Hannah Montana' Drove a 'Wedge' Between Him and Miley Cyrus
In a candid interview with GQ published in February 2011, Billy Ray Cyrus said co-starring with his daughter in "Hannah Montana" for four seasons took a toll on their relationship.
“The business was driving a wedge between us,” he said.
He also said he found it difficult to discipline his kids and later blamed himself for Miley Cyrus' highly publicized post-"Hannah Montana" rebelliousness.

“How many interviews did I give and say, ‘You know what’s important between me and Miley is I try to be a friend to my kids’? I said it a lot. And sometimes I would even read other parents might say, ‘You don’t need to be a friend, you need to be a parent.’ Well, I’m the first guy to say to them right now: You were right.'

"I should have been a better parent. I should have said, ‘Enough is enough — it’s getting dangerous and somebody’s going to get hurt.’ I should have, but I didn’t. Honestly, I didn’t know the ball was out of bounds until it was way up in the stands somewhere.”
Miley Cyrus, in a 2026 interview with Variety, credited her dad with "protecting her" on the set of the show.
“My dad was on set every single day,” Cyrus said. “So there was nothing that could happen that he wouldn’t know about.”
April 2022: Billy Ray Cyrus and Tish Cyrus Announce Divorce

After both she and Billy Ray Cyrus previously filed for divorce and then changed their minds, Tish Cyrus filed for divorce a third and final time in April 2022, according to court documents obtained by NBC News at the time.
The Cyrus family matriarch cited irreconcilable differences as grounds for the divorce and stated she and Billy Ray had not lived together for more than two years.
August 2023: Miley Cyrus Walks Tish Cyrus Down the Aisle When She Remarries
Miley Cyrus walked Tish Cyrus down the aisle when she tied the knot with "Prison Break" star Dominic Purcell in August 2023, Vogue reported.
“To see my mom this happy and in love is very emotional for me,” Miley Cyrus told the magazine. “Dom and my mom share the sweetest, most genuine love. It’s almost like it’s for the first time... "
"To stand beside my mother and give her away to Dom," she added, "is a moment I will cherish for the rest of my life.”
October 2023: Miley Cyrus Skips Billy Ray Cyrus' Wedding with Firehose
When Billy Ray Cyrus married Australian musician Firehose in October 2023, Miley Cyrus did not attend the ceremony.
The couple split less than a year later.
February 2024: Miley Cyrus Does Not Mention Billy Ray Cyrus in Grammy Speech
In February 2024, Miley Cyrus won the first two Grammys of her music career. While accepting the award for record of the year, she declined to thank her father in her speech.
The singer gave credit to her collaborators and her team along with her “main gays because look how good I look!”
She also thanked her mom, her sister, and her now-fiancé Maxx Morando but did not mention Billy Ray Cyrus in her speech.
“All the people that we love. I don’t think I’ve forgotten anyone,” she concluded. “But I might have forgotten underwear. Bye!”
The singer also did not mention her dad in a follow-up post about the Grammys she shared on Instagram.
June 7, 2024: Billy Ray Shares One of His 'Best Memories' with Miley Cyrus
In June 2024, Billy Ray Cyrus shared a sweet throwback pic of him  holding Miley Cyrus as a baby on Instagram in honor of the CMA Fest.

“One of my best memories ever: @cma Fest, back when it was Fan Fair. That’s @mileycyrus and me, surrounded by thousands of incredible fans at the fairgrounds,” he wrote alongside the pic and images of a handwritten poem he wrote while at the musical festival.

"The next day, someone handed me this picture, and I wrote that poem right then on the bus," Cyrus continued before adding of his daughter, "I’m incredibly proud of her. She’s a survivor and a true artist. She learned early on to love and appreciate the fans who make everything possible.
"We both cherish the connection we have with our fans and are grateful for every single one of you!"
June 12, 2024: Miley Cyrus Credits Her Dad with Teaching Her How to Handle Fame
During a lengthy interview on David Letterman’s Netflix show, "My Next Guest Needs No Introduction," Miley Cyrus credited her dad with teaching her how to navigate stardom.
When asked if her dad was her hero, the singer demurred, calling her mother her hero — but said she was grateful to inherit his "genes."
"My dad has great hair and I got that," she said.
She added that she her dad had  "a foot on the ground" and still loves the "real," and "nature," she said, clasping her fist. "And he always did even when he was super famous."
“I’m grateful for being able to watch him ahead of me. He’s almost given me this map. And there’s a map of what to do and what not to do, and he’s guided me on both," she said.
She later called her dad a "creative" and an "artist in the way that his brain works." She said he "has always made me feel safer in my own mind because we’re very similar in our ideas."
“So I think a lot of his perspective on reality and on life, I’ve inherited from him, more so than the way that I was raised —which really, my mom raised me.”
May 9, 2025: Billy Ray Cyrus Shares Surprise Pic with Miley Cyrus
Billy Ray Cyrus surprised fans when he shared a photo featuring Miley Cyrus and Maxx Morando in honor of his son Braison Cyrus' birthday.
May 10, 2025: Miley Cyrus Addresses 'Challenges' in Relationship with Billy Ray Cyrus
Miley Cyrus addressed the speculation about her her complicated relationship with her dad in a statement in her Instagram story on May 10, 2025.

“My dad and I have had our challenges over the years. Now, in my thirties, family is my priority above all else,” she wrote at the time. “I’m at peace knowing bridges have been built and time has done a lot of healing. Grateful for the good health and love that flows through my family.”
May 31, 2025: Miley Cyrus Says Her Mom’s  ‘Hurt’ Affected Her Own Relationship with Billy Ray Cyrus
The same month, when Miley Cyrus spoke to The New York Times, she indicated that the “hurt’ her mother had suffered in her marriage to her father affected her feelings about him.

“As I’ve gotten older, I’m respecting my parents as individuals instead of as parents — because my mom’s really loved my dad for her whole life, and I think being married to someone in the music industry and not being a part of it is obviously really hard,” she said.
“And so I think I took on some of my mom’s hurt as my own because it hurt her more than it hurt me as an adult, and so I owned a lot of her pain.”
She added that it helped her to see her mother happy in her marriage to actor Dominic Purcell and her father in a new romance with model and actor Elizabeth Hurley.

“Now that my mom is so in love with my stepdad, who I completely adore, and now that my dad, I see him finding happiness, too — I can love them both as individuals instead of as a parental pairing,” she explained. “I’m being an adult about it.”
September 19, 2025: Miley Cyrus Releases 'Peace Offering' Song About Billy Ray Cyrus
Miley Cyrus shared a new song about mending her relationship with her dad called “Secrets,” on Instagram.
"This song was written as a peace offering for someone I had lost for a time but always loved. In my experience, forgiveness and freedom are one and the same,” she wrote.

In the song, which features musicians Lindsey Buckingham and Mick Fleetwood of Fleetwood Mac, which Billy Ray Cyrus has called his favorite band, Miley Cyrus sings, “Call off all your forces / A white flag in the war.”
The same month, the singer told NPR, “I wanted (Billy Ray Cyrus) to know that I’m grown now, and I’m a safe space for him. I’m as much a friend as I am his kid now."

“That’s what this song was really about for me," she added. "Being that place that my dad feels safe… if you don’t take my hand as I reach it out, I’m going to chase you because I want that relationship in my life.”
The gesture worked. “It really helped build a bridge between him and I and reconnecting with each other,” she said.
September 19, 2025: Billy Ray Cyrus Responds to Miley Cyrus' Song About Him

After hearing "Secrets," Billy Ray Cyrus responded on Instagram, writing, “Best birthday gift a dad could ever ask for,” he wrote in an Instagram post. “The memories we’ve made together mean the world to me, and seeing you soar with your music makes me prouder every single day.”

title: 'The Pitt' Star Shawn Hatosy Says 'Nuanced' Dynamic Between Abbot and Mohan Was Planned From the 'Get Go'
summary: Shawn Hatosy speaks about season 2 of The Pitt, his character Jack Abbot and his dynamic with Mohan and more.
story: Shawn Hatosy made a heart-stopping entrance in Season 2 of "The Pitt" hours before his character's designated shift start time.
Dressed in a full S.W.A.T. uniform, Hatosy's character, Dr. Jack Abbot, stormed into the Pittsburgh Trauma Medical Center caring for a fallen comrade while nursing a graze wound of his own.

It's a far cry from his character's introduction in Season 1, when he's found standing on the roof of the hospital at the end of the night shift, requiring Dr. Robby (Noah Wyle) to quite literally talk him off the ledge.
Or is it?

"I believe Abbot is in the process of doing the work," Hatosy tells TODAY.com of his character in Season 2, which is set during a Fourth of July shift. "I imagine he had a conversation with his therapist ... and the therapist said, 'You need a hobby.'"
"From that, he decided that being a SWAT medic was appropriate, and he believes he's in the process of doing the work, but in many ways, it's a death wish," he adds.

Speaking with TODAY.com after Episode 11 of "The Pitt" aired March 19, Hatosy previews what's ahead for his character in Season 2, that "flirtatious" scene between Dr. Mohan and Dr. Abbot and his role opposite Sarah Michelle Gellar in "Ready or Not 2: Here I Come."

Abbot's approach to to leadership is so different to Robby's. You know, it's such a fascinating character because his presence is always kind of lingering, even though, to be quite honest, he's not there that much, right? It's a fantastic part. I get to show up, contemplate my existence, and then flirt sometimes, and be charming. And then when the chaos is hitting, he's just this calming influence. So I really enjoy the way these characters are drawn. They're kind of mirrors. They're very similar in stature. But they challenge each other.

That's a good point about McKay, because, she's certainly a character who is prime for — in my opinion, I don't know what they're thinking — a leadership role. You know what I mean, she could. It would be a different approach to to how they handle things absolutely.

I believe Abbot is in process of doing the work. You know? I imagine he had a conversation with his therapist — which he's been trying to get Robby to talk to, as we learned at the end of Season 1 — and the therapist said, 'You need a hobby. You need go play golf, go play pickleball or something.' And he from that decided that being a SWAT medic was appropriate. He believes he's in process doing the work. But in many ways, it's a death wish.

He’s so addicted to the adrenaline that it’s not really too different from what we’re seeing with Dr. Robby. It is interesting how he comes in. I remember as Season 1 ended, I wasn’t totally sure what they had in mind for Abbot in Season 2. I didn’t know, maybe they were going to bring him in full time, and at a certain point, I talked to Scott Gemmill, our showrunner, and he said, ‘Well, we think you’re going to come back around Episode 7,’ and I was terrified because I’m the night shift attending, I should be handing things off. I thought, oh gosh, it’s Fourth of July, how is Abbot going to show up? Is he on a gurney? Did he jump off the roof? A lot of questions were asked.

The writers from the get go, were layering subtle (hints of) that dynamic from the beginning, and so it was definitely discussed. But I think that the scene in Episode 7, where they're in the room together, and he's fixing himself, I just really enjoyed how that dynamic is nuanced and subtle, and it really asks more questions than it answers.

I really enjoy how she says something, and he says, "No, don't worry about me." And she's not. (Laughs.) She's just worried about her patient, which is what her DNA is. She's in a tough spot, and he's, in his own Abbot way, providing help and leadership.
I think that Dr. Abbot would say to Mohan, "Are you crazy? You're the future of medicine."
It's a bloody good time.

There's a dysfunction to it, but it's so enjoyable and delicious. There's tons of blood, there's suspense, there's mystery, there's this game that they're playing where they're trying to kill Grace (Samara Weaving) and Faith (Kathryn Newton). And there's characters that have a lot of different point of views. Mine being one who, you know, he's a bit of an imbecile. He comes from a family that has a lot of wealth and power and that created this very insufferable, infantile human who sort of has been given everything that he's ever wanted and anything that wasn't given to him, he took. And as power is dangled to him, the opportunity to have it for himself, we see how far he'll go and and who he becomes.

It's exciting, right? As actors, we love to be challenged, and we get the opportunity to display different dimensions, and these two are polar opposites, so it's fun. I'm really excited about "Ready or Not 2." I got to see it with an audience, which I encourage everyone to do, because it's nice to get back into a cinema and have that experience with a community of people who like that kind of film.

title: Hallmark Is Going on Tour! See the Dates and Stars Hitting the Road
summary: Hallmark stars are going on tour this summer! See tour dates, the stars attending and how to get tickets.
story: Hallmark fans are in for a treat! The network will soon embark on its first-ever tour, where attendees will get to meet the stars from some of their favorite TV movies in person.

“Hallmark Stars Live” will launch this summer, the network and Gershwin Entertainment announced March 24 on TODAY. The tour will be hosted by “A Grand Ole Opry Christmas” actor Nikki DeLoach and “feature Hallmark mainstays for a night where the cameras are off — and the stories keep rolling,” according to a press release. The retailer Michaels is also a presenting partner for the tour.

DeLoach and other stars will hop from city to city for two weeks — from July 22 to Aug. 2 — where they will bring “a little Hallmark movie magic” to the audience during a 90-minute event. But unlike Hallmark’s films, the events will be entirely unscripted. At each of the 10 stops, the stars will share behind-the-scenes stories from their time on set and answer questions from fans.

“At Hallmark, we’re always exploring new ways to connect with fans and bring our brand to life, so we’re thrilled to introduce ‘Hallmark Stars Live’ as our newest immersive experience,” said Lindsey Roy, senior vice president of branded experiences at Hallmark. “For everyone who’s asked us to bring the Hallmark joy they love a little closer to home, this tour is for you.”
Read on to find out everything you need to know about the "Hallmark Stars Live" tour.
Which Stars Are Joining the Hallmark Tour?

title: 'Mormon Wives' Star Jessi Ngatikaura's Estranged Husband, Jordan, Files a Restraining Order Against Her
summary: “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star Jessi Draper Ngatikaura is facing a restraining order from estranged husband Jordan Ngatikaura.
story: “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star Jessi Draper Ngatikaura is facing a restraining order from estranged husband Jordan Ngatikaura.
In a court document obtained by NBC News, Jordan Ngatikaura filed for a temporary restraining order on March 20. The reality star filed an opposition the same day.
Jessi Ngatikaura's attorneys declined to comment. Reps for Jordan Ngatikaura did not immediately respond to NBC News' request for comment.
The request came a day after Jordan Ngatikaura filed for divorce, according to a court document obtained by NBC News.

“This has not been an easy decision and it comes with a heavy heart,” Jordan Ngatikaura said in a statement to People following the divorce filing. “I’m grateful for the shared memories and the lessons. While our paths are now moving in different directions, my priority remains my children and ensuring they feel loved, supported, and protected through this transition. I am committed to handling this next chapter with kindness and respect.”
The couple met at a birthday party in 2019, he shared in an episode of the “Weekly Trash” podcast, and got married in 2020.
Jordan Ngatikaura has a daughter, Peyton, from a previous marriage. He and the “Mormon Wives” star, who has also previously been married, share two children, Jovi and Jagger.

The couple’s marital issues have been documented on the Hulu reality show, including infidelity rumors and their 90-day separation. During Season 3, Jessi Ngatikaura revealed she had an “emotional affair” with “Vanderpump Villa” star Marciano Brunette, which led to the couple’s decision to be open and honest on camera about their personal lives.

“When we started talking about it on camera, we both agreed that if we’re going to do this, we’re going to do it 100%. We’re going to own our s---. We’re going to talk about the mistakes we’ve made and take the audience along on the journey,” she told TODAY.com in November 2025.
Now in its fourth season, which was released on March 12, viewers had been curious if the two were still together.

“We still have ups and downs and hard days and, you know, easier days. But that’s just real life,” Jessi Ngatikaura told “Entertainment Tonight” about her relationship with her husband, sharing that she felt it was “important to share” all parts of her life and marriage.

At the time of her interview, she told the outlet, “We’re together. We’ve been back together for almost a year. We’re still working on it. We’re still in therapy. It’s still easy and hard at the same time.”
She also said that she can't easily walk away from her marriage despite their issues because of their children, as well as financial reasons.
When joking about needing to protect her newfound empire,  Jessi Ngatikaura said, “Yeah, prenups get ‘em.”

title: Why Dionne Warwick Hand Picked Teyana Taylor to Play Her in Biopic
summary: Dionne Warwick is gushing over Teyana Taylor, who is set to portray the music icon in an upcoming biopic.
story: Dionne Warwick is gushing over Teyana Taylor, who is set to portray the music icon in an upcoming biopic.

On Monday, March 23, Warwick stopped by TODAY to chat about her new album, “DWuets,” which will be her last. Over the past few weeks, the legendary singer has been sharing details about the record on social media, including that Diane Warren wrote all the tracks and that it features duets with Cynthia Erivo and Kehlani.
While on TODAY, Warwick spoke to Al Roker, Dylan Dreyer and Craig Melvin about her new single, “Ocean in the Desert” with Erivo, before talking about another project she has in the works: a biopic.
Al mentioned that the “I Say a Little Prayer for You” singer “hand-picked” Taylor to play her in the movie.
The singer recalled that her son Damon Elliott sent her a photograph of the “One Battle After Another” star side-by-side with a throwback picture of Warwick.
“I said, ‘Oh my God, it’s not me?’ I thought ‘I’m going to talk to my daddy about this one. Wait a minute,’” Warwick joked.
"Then I met her," she added. "She’s such a personable young lady and very talented. She was very excited about even being thought of to do it.”

The TODAY co-hosts then showed Warwick a clip of Taylor’s sit-down with Craig on TODAY in January where she discussed the role and how she plans to pull it off. At the time, Craig asked if Taylor intends to sing in the film.
“Yeah, but hopefully I’m lip-syncing. Because, baby, Dionne Warwick, that’s a whole different level of voice. I got pipes but she’s got pipes," Taylor responded.
(Later in that interview, Taylor predicted she might go the same route as Angela Bassett in “What’s Love Got to Do With It” and mouth along to Warwick’s recorded vocals.)
Warwick and the co-hosts laughed before the icon said she would support Taylor lip-syncing.
“If that’s what she wants to do,” Warwick said in between giggles. “She is so cute. That’s all I can think of … and she says what she means.”
“That really surprised me," she added. "I thought, ‘Oh God, she is me.’”

Warwick frequently compliments Taylor on social media, recently applauding the actor on X after she earned her first Oscar nomination at the 98th Academy Awards. “So proud of my baby @TEYANATAYLOR on so many levels,” she wrote.
“That’s my girl,” she said, in part. “You go dear!!!”
Taylor has been attached to the Warwick movie for years. She first mentioned they were in the “building process” for the film in April 2023.
During Taylor’s TODAY interview in January 2026, she explained why the film has been in development for years.

“That is my lady. She’s so amazing,” Taylor told Craig. “I’m just so happy to make her proud all while also just being very protective of her, making sure we have the perfect story. She has so much career … I know it’s taken a little while but we just want it to be perfect. And I want it to be perfect for her.”

title: Ariel Sullivan Says ‘Beneath,’ Her Next Book, Is ‘Darker’ Than ‘Conform.’ Read a Preview
summary: Ariel Sullivan is back with another novel. She previews "Beneath," the start of a new trilogy set hundreds of years before the events of "Conform."
story: Six months after her debut novel "Conform" was published, Ariel Sullivan is back with another novel.
"Beneath," out March 24, is the start of a new trilogy that takes place 200 years before the stratified world of "Conform" was created, laying the groundwork for how the dystopia came to be.
Both novels are published through Jenna Bush Hager's Thousand Voices x Random House Publishing Group imprint.

"It's a breathtaking dystopian romance that shows how love can grow even in the darkest places. It's about found family, resilience and what happens when people start to question the rules they've been living by," Jenna said of "Beneath" on TODAY March 23.

"Beneath" takes place about seven years after a world war causes the global order's collapse. Sasha, our main character, is living in an underground society with other survivors. It's a grittier world than Emeline's in "Conform."

"'Conform' and 'Beneath' trilogies are the same coin, different sides. 'Conform' is that shiny side. 'Beneath' is hidden and corroded. It's emotional and character-driven. It's darker. It's grittier. So anything that you expected from 'Conform,' don't expect it in 'Beneath,'" Sullivan tells TODAY.com.
The idea for "Beneath" came when she was writing backstory for "Conform."
"I was working on world-building and writing about why things were the way they were. Sasha showed up. She just stomped into the picture and sat down," she says.

Sullivan says, in a creative burst, she burned the dinner she was making that night. "We ordered pizza, and I drafted those three books, like, very roughly, but like, drafted them out. And I was like, 'This is what has to happen,'" she says.

Keeping with the novel's "darker" themes, Sullivan teases that the novel's core group of characters — Unit Seven, a squadron of elite soldiers that Sasha ends up joining for an Earth-saving mission — each represent one of the seven stages of grief.

"You've got shock and denial and anger and bargaining and acceptance. It'll be interesting if readers can find out which characters are what," Sullivan says, adding that she'll eventually reveal who is who, but not yet.

After "Beneath," Sullivan will release the sequel to "Conform," which will take place right where we left off with Emeline. There's also a third trilogy that Sullivan is waiting to share details about.
But for now, we're diving "Beneath." Check out an excerpt below.
Excerpt from Chapter 15 of 'Beneath'

In the following excerpt, Sasha Cadell is about to step foot onto the Earth’s surface, now a nuclear wasteland, for the first time after living underground for six years. But she’s not alone. Unit Seven, a squadron of elite soldiers, are right behind her, performing one final check of their radiation-protective gear before embarking into unknown terrain in pursuit of Mission Hope — the key to humanity’s survival…
“Audio, Cadell.”
Sweat dripped down my back. “Check.”
“Night vision,” Patrick called out.
Everyone hit buttons on the left arm of their suits. I stared at the series of buttons Levi had given me a rundown on and hit mine. The shield of my helmet shifted to night vision.
“Check.” I clicked it off.
“Navigation systems.”
I pressed the next button, and a map appeared with eight dots showing our location. “Check.”
“Partner communication.”
I hit another button, and Levi’s dry voice filled my head. “Last chance to run, Sasha.”
“I’m not running,” I told him, even as my heart battered against the plates of my gear. He nodded at me, and we switched back to the unit channel. “Check.”
“Oxygen level,” Patrick prompted.
I hit the last button, and my oxygen level projected on the helmet shield. I didn’t know if I would adjust to the helmet. I responded, “Hundred percent.”

“Mission is a go, repeat, mission is a go.” Patrick gave a thumbs-up to Jaxon and the other member of his unit who stood at the shack. I finally looked at the shack itself just once. I hadn’t seen the small hut in almost six years, when I had sprinted to it, demanding care for Lara, barely clinging to life in my arms. I closed my eyes at the memory.

Jaxon hit a button, and the steel door began to close on the shack and all of Haven, the only home I had known since the war. My heart hit my throat as the man called out, “Door closing; Explor­atory Unit Seven is in the holding box.”

Levi tightened the straps of the med bag. Rumi and Patrick danced around each other; whether they talked or simply moved off muscle memory and deep recognition of each other, I didn’t know. Tristian and Ingrid both moved, but it wasn’t coordinated. Multiple times, Tristian’s helmet swiveled in the direction of Levi, his partner of seven years. Guilt settled in my gut at the partnership I had ended. Damien and Isla began executing an elaborate handshake—a fist-pounding, hip-bumping dance that ended in a bow.
Levi’s voice filled my helmet. “Just us. You good?”
“Would it matter if I said no?” I asked, my gloved hands sweaty.
“Yes, it would matter to me,” Levi said, his helmet shield shifting to me until I saw my reflection. Surprise hit me at the formidable force I beheld in his shield.
“Door’s shut. Switching communication to the group channel,” Levi told me.
Tristian cleared his throat, and everyone fell silent. They drifted toward him, forming a circle of sorts. “There’s no one else I’d rather do this with. You know our mission.”

Patrick shifted his weight between the balls of his feet, Rumi si­lent next to him. Isla and Damien bumped lightly into each other. Ingrid was stiff. Levi was a steady presence next to me, the arm of his suit resting against mine. For once I welcomed the contact.
“You ready, Cadell?” Everyone’s helmets were directed at me, and my heart picked up its pace. Could they hear its frantic beating over the communication system?
“Yes,” I said breathlessly. I had to be.
Tristian’s helmet met mine. “Good.”
I wondered what he hid behind his helmet. Did Tristian smile, or did he hear the fear in my voice?

A hissing began behind us, large metal doors slowly peeling apart. I was going above. The realization slammed into me as the light started to peek through the growing crack. The temperature reading began to drop as cold air slithered in. Fresh air. The metal doors ground open.
We’re almost there, Lara; hang on. We’re almost there.
Lara’s pale, too thin form heavy in my hands, slowing me down as I attempted to sprint. The entrance coming into view.
It’s okay, Sash. Go on without me.
No, I will not leave you. You have to hang on. They’ll help you. They’ll save you.
It’s okay. I’ll be with Mom, Dad, and Eli. I’m ready. I’m not scared. I’m ready.
No, no. You have to stay with me.
Her eyes closed.
Lara, don’t leave me. Stay with me. I’m not ready. I’m not ready. Help me.
Blinding lights forced my eyes closed, so all I saw was her pale face. She wouldn’t grow to be beautiful.
“As long as there’s life,” Tristian called out to the group.
“We defend it with our own,” Unit Seven answered.
I opened my eyes. There was still life. Not my family, but there was life. I couldn’t fail again.
“We go out as one,” Tristian continued. Time froze as the bright light framed his massive form. His back to the world above. His focus on his unit.
A chill ran up my spine as Unit Seven answered:
“We come back as one.”

Excerpted from Beneath by Ariel Sullivan. Copyright © 2026 by Ariel Sullivan. All rights reserved. No part of this excerpt may be reproduced or reprinted without permission in writing from the publisher.

title: ‘Chuck Norris Facts’ Creator Explains How a Ben Stiller Movie Led to the Classic Meme
summary: Chuck Norris has died at 86. Before then, he was a defining part of the internet. Creator Ian Spector tells TODAY.com about how Chuck Norris Facts came to be.
story: There was probably a time when it sounded strange: Chuck Norris, Internet legend.
Yes, the martial arts icon and action star of the small and big screen. That Chuck Norris.

The “Walker, Texas Ranger” star, whose family announced March 20 he died at the age of 86, would grow to become as much a part of the Internet era as waiting in a queue for Taylor Swift concert tickets, thanks to memes centered around the actor that Ian Spector started more than two decades ago.
“I was just a teenager who had a website on a random little domain name,” Spector tells TODAY.com about his own website.

The memes, of course, are those “Chuck Norris facts” you’ve probably seen that amplify how tough Norris was — something like, “In an emergency, Chuck Norris doesn’t call 911 ... 911 calls Chuck Norris.” Or, “Chuck Norris doesn't wear a watch. He decides what time it is.” Or, “A bulletproof vest wears Chuck Norris for protection.” You get the idea.

The “facts” originated in 2005 after Spector noticed a forum on a website called Something Awful that presented fake facts about Vin Diesel, he recalled to his alma mater's newspaper in 2006. Spector wanted to create a site that gathered all of these “facts,” and allow people to submit their own. He created a “fact generator” and shared the link on Something Awful.
“I never expected any of this to ever take off, right? Like I posted a link in a forum once and the next morning, there were 10,000 hits, and (it) grew from there,” Spector says.

Spector then created a poll seeking to find a new celebrity for people to target with their “facts.” Norris was the clear winner. To put that in terms of the subject at hand, the response proved that the Chuck Norris didn’t need the Internet — the Internet needed Chuck Norris.
Slightly more than 20 years ago, the idea of a meme — a type of iterative image, text, video or concept that could spread organically on the internet — took off.
Spector believes Norris himself set the stage for the meme’s popularity, thanks to his appearance in the 2004 Ben Stiller movie, “Dodgeball.”

“I think that also was a kind of catalyst, because there were people who knew and loved his work from the ‘70s and ‘80s, and ‘Walker, Texas Ranger’ in the ‘90s, and there’s this brand new audience of people who kind of just knew him as the guy that saved the day for the winning team in ‘Dodgeball,’ and that kind of grew into its own ecology,” he says.
“I chalk it up to he also is just incredibly consistent in terms of his values and where he stands. Like, you know what you’re getting with Chuck Norris.”
Spector would go on to write five books of facts about Norris. The first one, “The Truth About Chuck Norris: 400 Facts About the World’s Greatest Human,” was a New York Times bestseller.
It was a full-blown phenomenon, turning Norris into a quasi-Paul Bunyan larger than life character. It all begs the question: Did the meme concept itself originate with Norris?
“The short answer is, yeah, and my ego likes to think so,” says Spector, while adding that different types of online humor may have existed “before my time.”
Spector also points out that “people weren’t just writing text over images quite yet” when Chuck Norris facts exploded onto the scene.
“But the meme as a form of literacy, or the form like ‘here’s the template for a joke’ that we all get and everybody gets to participate in’ sprouted from the facts, he says.
Spector says Norris “holds a special place in Internet culture.”
“I’m glad that the world was able to find all the amazing greatness inside of Chuck Norris, something that could brighten somebody’s day,” he says.
“I’m glad that the world was able to find all the amazing greatness inside of Chuck Norris, something that could brighten somebody’s day."
Norris facts live on. There is a Chuck Norris facts website that Spector is not affiliated with that exists today, which speaks to its enduring relevance.
“I think it worked, because he was that guy, like he had that toughness and disciplining career. I kind of just built the joke system around that,” he says.

Spector, who says he and Norris had a “nice and surreal meeting” in 2006, credits the star and his wife, Gena O’Kelley, for “being incredible for running with a joke.” Indeed, Norris embraced the mania, reading some of the facts on TV and writing his own book of factts.

Norris’ legacy was already written when a new period about his life as an Internet star would be carved, in large part, by Spector. And perhaps no other further proof is needed than in the outpouring of inquiries Spector received following Norris’ death.
“Yeah, there’s been quite a lot of interest from around the world,” he says.

title: What Happened with Chappell Roan in Brazil? Singer Responds to Allegations Her Security Made a Child Cry
summary: Chappell Roan has denied that a member of her security team scolded the 11-year-old stepdaughter of soccer star Jorginho Frello.
story: Chappell Roan has denied that a member of her security team scolded an 11-year-old girl and her mother at a hotel after the girl's stepfather, an Italian-Brazilian soccer star, wrote a message chastising Roan and accusing a security guard of reducing the girl to tears.

The pop star responded in a video to her Instagram story on March 22, one day after soccer player Jorginho Frello posted on his Instagram that a security guard had a "disproportionate" response to his stepdaughter walking by Roan's table at a hotel.
People ranging from Frello's wife to the mayor of Rio de Janeiro has since weighed in on the story. Here's what to know about the alleged controversy.
Who Are Jorginho Frello, Catherine Harding and Chappell Roan?
Roan, 28, won the Best New Artist Grammy in 2025 and is known for hits like "Hot to Go!" and "Pink Pony Club."
At the time of the alleged incident, Roan was in São Paulo to perform at the popular Lollapalooza Brazil music festival.

Frello is known as Jorginho, a soccer star who has played for the Italian national team and currently plays midfielder for Clube de Regatas do Flamengo, a professional team that is part of Brazil's highest-level soccer league. He previously played for two of the best-known teams in the world, Chelsea and Arsenal, in the English Premier League.
Harding is an Irish singer who is Frello's wife and the mother of the 11-year-old girl. She was with her during the time of the alleged incident.
Harding shares daughter Ada with British actor Jude Law, a father of seven who was not present at the hotel during the alleged encounter.
Why Did Jorginho Call Out Chappell Roan?

Frello posted a lengthy message on his Instagram story on March 21 — which was re-shared on X by Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Cavaliere — accusing a member of Roan's security team of berating his wife and stepdaughter and leaving the girl in tears.
Frello said that Harding and Ada were coincidentally staying at the same hotel as Roan and had planned on attending her show in São Paulo that night because his stepdaughter is a big fan.
He said that the girl thought she spotted Roan while she was eating breakfast with Harding, so she went to see if it was her.
"She simply walked past the singer's table, looked to confirm it was her, smiled, and went back to sit with her mum," Frello wrote. "She didn't say anything, didn't ask for anything."
Frello then said a large security guard approached Harding and their daughter and had a response that was "completely disproportionate."

He claims the security guard told his wife that she shouldn't allow her daughter to "disrespect" or "harass" other people. Frello said the security guard threatened to file a complaint with the hotel as his stepdaughter sat there "in tears."
Frello said Ada was "completely shaken" and "cried a lot." He then directed a message to Roan.
"WITHOUT YOUR FANS, YOU WOULD BE NOTHING," he wrote. "AND TO THE FANS, SHE DOES NOT DESERVE YOUR AFFECTION."
How Did Chappell Roan Respond?
Roan shared a video on her Instagram story of her lying in bed and reacting to Frello's message.

"I'm just going to tell my half of the story of what happened today with a mother and child who were involved with a security guard who is not my personal security," she said. "I didn't even see a woman and a child. No one came up to me. No one bothered me. I was just sitting at breakfast in my hotel."
"I did not ask the security guard to go up and talk to this mother and child," she continued. "They did not come up to me. They weren't doing anything."
Roan has previously called out how some fans have treated her. In 2024, she wrote an Instagram post about "harassment" and "predatory behavior" she said she has experienced from superfans.
However, she clarified that stance in her video message in response to Frello's post.

"I do not hate people who are fans of my music," she said. "I do not hate children. That is crazy. I'm sorry to the mother and child that someone was assuming something that you would do something, that if you felt uncomfortable, that makes me really sad. You did not deserve that."
What Did Catherine Harding Say?
Harding shared her side of the encounter in an Instagram video on March 22, saying that she was “shocked” at what happened.

“I know that Chappell has responded saying that it wasn’t her security, and she didn’t do it,” Harding said. “So, 100% this security guard was not a security guard of the hotel, that’s what I can say. He looks after artists. So I don’t know if it was her personal security guard, but he was with her, so that is all I know.”
Harding said she and Ada came to São Paulo specifically to see Roan’s show because her daughter wanted tickets as a birthday present.
She reiterated her husband’s version of events that her daughter simply walked by Roan’s table to see if it was her but did not have her phone, did not take any photos and did not approach Roan.

A “very large” and “intimidating” security guard then approached their table and scolded her by saying she needed to teach her daughter to respect other’s privacy, according to Harding. She said she tried to explain to the man that her daughter is around celebrities frequently and “would never cross someone’s boundaries.”

“I was shocked,” Harding said. “I said to (the security guard) at the time, if it’s the case that she doesn’t want people to even look at her, she is very welcome to eat her breakfast in her room because this here is a public area, and I am a paying guest as well as her, so if I want to walk past someone’s table and look at them, I have every right to do so. I am not approaching her, I am not doing anything to make her feel intimidated or nothing.”
Harding added that she and her daughter ended up not going to Roan’s show.
“I hope that maybe if it wasn’t her that she learns not to allow people that work for her to treat people like this,” Harding said.
Why Did the Mayor of Rio Weigh In?
In the wake of Frello's initial Instagram post, the mayor of Rio de Janeiro wrote on X in Portuguese that he will ban Roan from playing one of Rio's biggest music festivals.

"I mean that as long as I’m in charge of our city — this young lady @ChappellRoan will never perform at Todo Mundo no Rio!" Eduardo Cavaliere wrote, according to the English translation on X. "I doubt that Shakira @shakira @Shakira_Brasil would do that! By the way, @FrelloJorginho your little one is already the guest of honor from the organization in May!"
Frello responded to the post with a pair of heart emojis.

Todo Mundo no Rio is a set of massive free concerts in May that are held on Copacabana Beach. Colombian superstar Shakira is headlining this year's events, and Madonna and Lady Gaga have performed at previous editions.

title: All About Sheinelle Jones’ Book Tour and How to Preorder ‘Through Mom’s Eyes’
summary: Learn about Sheinelle Jones' debut book "Through Mom's Eyes," how to preorder and a list of Sheinelle's book tour dates.
story: Sheinelle Jones is gearing up for the release of her debut book and her first-ever book tour this spring.
“Through Mom’s Eyes,” out officially on April 14, is a collection of life lessons shared by women who raised extraordinary people and encouraged by her late husband, Uche Ojeh.
“‘Through Mom’s Eyes’ celebrates the quiet, everyday love that shapes who we become,” her publisher, Putnam Books, said in a press release.

TODAY viewers may recognize the title of “Through Mom’s Eyes” as it was the name of Sheinelle’s very own TODAY series, in which she interviewed moms of celebrities and public figures. In each installment, she picked their brains on their parenting styles and tips and even used their advice to help raise her three children. She shares her kids with her late husband of 17 years, Uche Ojeh, who passed away last year after a battle with brain cancer.
The book “Through Mom’s Eyes” features the insights Sheinelle has learned from dozens of women, mixed with stories from Sheinelle’s journey of finding light through her grief.
Putnam also announced that Sheinelle will celebrate her book’s arrival with a tour that includes special guests, like Al Roker.
See the list of stops and more details below.

title: Lea Michele Reveals the Special Mementos She Keeps in Her Dressing Room at 'Chess' Theater
summary: Lea Michele has kept a few very special mementos backstage with her while while starring in the hit Broadway revival of the 1986 musical "Chess."On the March 22 episode of Sunday Sitdown, the former "Glee" star took TODAY's Willie Geist on a tour of the musical's home, the Imperial Theatre, the same venue where Michele made her Broadway debut as an 8-year-old actor in the original Broadway run of "Les Misérables.""I found a picture of me from 1995 when I was in 'Les Mis,' and it was me and righ
story: Lea Michele has kept a few very special mementos backstage with her while while starring in the hit Broadway revival of the 1986 musical "Chess."

On the March 22 episode of Sunday Sitdown, the former "Glee" star took TODAY's Willie Geist on a tour of the musical's home, the Imperial Theatre, the same venue where Michele made her Broadway debut as an 8-year-old actor in the original Broadway run of "Les Misérables."

"I found a picture of me from 1995 when I was in 'Les Mis,' and it was me and right behind me was a 'Chess' playbill," Michele told Willie. "And then the first time I walked into this theater with 'Chess,' I looked right here, and here is where I took that picture."

The 39-year-old entertainer, who dazzled audiences after taking over the lead role in the 2022 revival of "Funny Girl," has kept the throwback snapshot of her in "Les Misérables" in her dressing room since "Chess" debuted in November 2025.
“I remember saying to my parents after my first performance (in 'Les Mis'), ‘I want to do this for the rest of my life. Don’t ever tell me to stop. Don’t ever let me stop,’” she said.
The photo serves as a reminder to Michele, who grew up in the Bronx and New Jersey the daughter of a nurse and a deli owner, of how she made her wildest dreams come true.

"I look at this picture every day before I go out there, and I’m like, I think she would be so happy, and she would be so grateful that I listened to her when she said don’t ever let me stop doing this," she said.
Also in Michele's dressing room: drawings her own little ones, son Ever, 5, and daughter Emery, 1, made for her.

"I brought my children here the other day. My son he’s a little, like second show, you know? He was around for 'Funny Girl,'" she told Willie. "And my daughter was just running around here with this confidence that I was like, 'OK, I see you.'"
"But I looked at my mom, and my mom was like, 'I can’t believe I’m here with your children and I used to walk you through that door,'" she said.
"You know, my son is gonna be 6, so it’s only two years younger than I was. So it’s a real incredible moment for my family," she added.

In "Chess," Michele plays Florence Vassy, a brilliant Cold War-era chess strategist who becomes entangled in a love triangle with an American chess master and his Russian rival, played, respectively, by Aaron Tveit and Nicholas Christopher.
The musical features music by ABBA members Benny Andersson and Björn Ulvaeus, lyrics by Tim Rice of “Evita” fame and a new book by Danny Strong ("Empire").
Michele describes the role of the passionate Florence as her first "woman" character.

"This is the first real adult that I’ve ever played before," she told Geist. "I can’t wait to get on this stage, and be Florence, and to feel that strength and that confidence, and she’s living in this world of men, and what does that mean? And I just love it."

title: How to Watch 'Saturday Night Live UK’ in the U.S.
summary: The British version of "Saturday Night Live" will be available to stream on Peacock, beginning March 22.
story: Live from the other side of the pond … it’s Saturday night.
The new U.K. version of "Saturday Night Live," premieringi n March, will follow the blueprint of its American counterpart, with guest hosts and live musical guests.
In fact, “SNL” legend Tina Fey will host the premiere episode, and even starred in a cheeky promo with the cast, in which she did a Mary Poppins impression while plugging her appearance.

“‘Saturday Night Live’ is one of the most influential comedy brands in the world and bringing the UK edition to Peacock is a natural extension of that legacy,” NBCUniversal Entertainment chief business officer Liz Jenkins said in a statement about the show.
“As the streaming home of ‘SNL,’ Peacock is proud to introduce bold new British voices to a global stage.”

“Saturday Night Live UK” will kick off its first season with cast members Hammed Animashaun, Ayoade Bamgboye, Larry Dean, Celeste Dring, George Fouracres, Ania Magliano, Annabel Marlow, Al Nash, Jack Shep, Emma Sidi and Paddy Young.
